name: Feed the Shadow
tips: A Common Rat destroyed for 2 mana nukes a 10 🤍 target — one of the cheapest removal lines in the game.
Your Dark Matter pool is added on top of the destroyed ally's 🗡️ — with a big pool even a small sacrifice hits like a truck.
Eclipse Shade is perfect Feed fodder: 20 🗡️ becomes 20+ damage before the self-burn eats her — just remember the fed Shade stays dead even if the spell is negated.
The pool gain (+1 Dark Matter per friendly Dark Mage) is easy to miss — chain Feed the Shadow with Dark Matter Stash to grow the pool fast.
Don't over-feed. Giving up a 5-mana minion for 2-mana removal rarely trades up unless the enemy target is a game-winner.
rulings: The damage equals the destroyed ally's total 🗡️ (base + attack_bonus) plus YOUR Dark Matter pool at resolution (after the react window closes).

Destroying the ally is a Cost, not an effect — it resolves before the damage and before the Dark Matter gain. If the destroyed ally was a Dark Mage, it is no longer on the board when the gain counts Dark Mages.
If you have no valid ally to destroy, Feed the Shadow cannot be cast.

The gain credits YOUR pool: +1 Dark Matter per friendly [[Dark Mage]] on the board at resolution (element Dark AND tribe exactly Mage), including one summoned mid-chain by another effect. The gain resolves after the damage, so it does not scale THIS cast.
